article_meta.title: By Jonah Goldberg July 11, 2016 11:14 AM PT Share via Close extra sharing options Email Facebook X LinkedIn Threads Redd

raw_response: {"detections":[{"biasType":"Spin","biasedText":"knocked the lazy certainty out of almost everybody","explanation":"The phrase 'lazy certainty' is an interpretive, editorializing characterization applied broadly to people on both sides of these issues, implying their prior convictions were intellectually slothful rather than sincerely held."},{"biasType":"Opinion Statements Presented as Fact","biasedText":"\"National conversations\" are usually efforts to bully everyone into accepting a single narrative","explanation":"This is a subjective judgment presented as a factual observation. Whether national conversations are 'bullying' efforts is a contested opinion, not an established fact."},{"biasType":"Mudslinging/Ad Hominem","biasedText":"Former New York City Mayor Rudy Giuliani (who did not lose his lazy certainty)","explanation":"The parenthetical remark is a personal insult directed at Giuliani, characterizing him as intellectually lazy in contrast to others who showed more nuance."},{"biasType":"Opinion Statements Presented as Fact","biasedText":"That's true, and tragic, and fairly irrelevant","explanation":"Dismissing Giuliani's point about black-on-black crime as 'fairly irrelevant' is a subjective judgment presented as fact. Whether this statistic is relevant to the debate is contested."},{"biasType":"Spin","biasedText":"Liberals, for their part, are often rather obtuse in how they discuss policing","explanation":"The word 'obtuse' is a loaded, dismissive characterization of how liberals discuss policing, substituting an insult for neutral description."},{"biasType":"Opinion Statements Presented as Fact","biasedText":"many draw a line around cops, creating one of the strangest ironies of modern liberalism","explanation":"Characterizing this as 'one of the strangest ironies of modern liberalism' is a subjective editorial judgment presented as though it were an objective observation."},{"biasType":"Opinion Statements Presented as Fact","biasedText":"the first obligation of the state is to defend the safety and property of its citizens","explanation":"This is a particular philosophical and political position (associated with conservative/Hobbesian thought) presented as an uncontested fact. Others would argue the first obligation of the state includes protecting civil liberties or ensuring justice."}]}

detections.biasedText: knocked the lazy certainty out of almost everybody
detections.explanation: The phrase 'lazy certainty' is an interpretive, editorializing characterization applied broadly to people on both sides of these issues, implying their prior convictions were intellectually slothful rather than sincerely held.

detections.biasedText: "National conversations" are usually efforts to bully everyone into accepting a single narrative
detections.explanation: This is a subjective judgment presented as a factual observation. Whether national conversations are 'bullying' efforts is a contested opinion, not an established fact.

detections.biasedText: Former New York City Mayor Rudy Giuliani (who did not lose his lazy certainty)
detections.explanation: The parenthetical remark is a personal insult directed at Giuliani, characterizing him as intellectually lazy in contrast to others who showed more nuance.

detections.biasedText: That's true, and tragic, and fairly irrelevant
detections.explanation: Dismissing Giuliani's point about black-on-black crime as 'fairly irrelevant' is a subjective judgment presented as fact. Whether this statistic is relevant to the debate is contested.

detections.biasedText: Liberals, for their part, are often rather obtuse in how they discuss policing
detections.explanation: The word 'obtuse' is a loaded, dismissive characterization of how liberals discuss policing, substituting an insult for neutral description.

detections.biasedText: many draw a line around cops, creating one of the strangest ironies of modern liberalism
detections.explanation: Characterizing this as 'one of the strangest ironies of modern liberalism' is a subjective editorial judgment presented as though it were an objective observation.

detections.biasedText: the first obligation of the state is to defend the safety and property of its citizens
detections.explanation: This is a particular philosophical and political position (associated with conservative/Hobbesian thought) presented as an uncontested fact. Others would argue the first obligation of the state includes protecting civil liberties or ensuring justice.
